obsidian_markdown:
# RPL-based attack detection approaches in IoT networks: review and taxonomy

## Summary
This paper provides a comprehensive survey on attack detection approaches targeting the RPL protocol in IoT networks. It categorizes potential attacks, explores effective security mechanisms, identifies the most destructive threats to RPL functionality, and discusses the real-world implications of these security challenges. The paper emphasizes its unique contribution through a qualitative comparison with existing surveys and suggests areas for further research.

## Contributions
- Categorizes potential attacks targeting the RPL protocol based on their impact on network performance.
- Identifies the most destructive and problematic threats affecting RPL functionality.
- Provides a qualitative comparison with other surveys in the field to highlight the uniqueness of this study.

## Method
The study systematically reviews existing literature on RPL-based attack detection in IoT networks. It categorizes attacks, explores security mechanisms, and evaluates their effectiveness. The paper also includes a qualitative comparison with other surveys to underscore its unique contributions.

## Results
The survey identifies key security challenges and destructive threats to RPL in IoT networks. It provides insights into effective security mechanisms and highlights areas for future research.

## Limitations
The study intentionally focuses only on RPL-specific attacks and acknowledges this as a limitation. It suggests further research in areas not covered by the survey.

## Keywords
RPL, IoT networks, Intrusion detection system, Artificial intelligence, Routing protocol, Security, Attack detection, Survey
